prompt_preview:
You are a classical planning system. You are given a planning domain and a problem both in PDDL syntax.

STRICT REQUIREMENT: Your response must contain ONLY the executable plan — nothing else. No preamble, no explanation, no "We need to...", no natural-language commentary, no markdown unless it is a

message.content:
```pddl
(register_participant indexing_worker_1)
(bind_participant_to_endpoint indexing_worker_1 search_endpoint_1)
(attach_schema_to_participant indexing_worker_1 schema_v1)
(finalize_participant_registration indexing_worker_1 schema_v1)
(validate_source_shard_for_indexer indexing_worker_1 source_shard_a schema_v1)
(attach_credential_to_participant indexing_worker_1 operator_credential_1)
(claim_indexer_for_shard indexing_worker_1 source_shard_a operator_credential_1)
(detach_credential_from_participant indexing_worker_1 operator_credential_1)
(detach_schema_from_participant indexing_worker_1 schema_v1)
(register_participant query_worker_1)
(bind_participant_to_endpoint query_worker_1 search_endpoint_2)
(attach_schema_to_participant query_worker_1 schema_v1)
(finalize_participant_registration query_worker_1 schema_v1)
(prepare_target_shard_for_query_worker query_worker_1 target_shard_a schema_v1)
(attach_credential_to_participant query_worker_1 operator_credential_1)
(claim_query_worker_for_shard query_worker_1 target_shard_a operator_credential_1)
(detach_credential_from_participant query_worker_1 operator_credential_1)
(detach_schema_from_participant query_worker_1 schema_v1)
(provision_external_index indexing_worker_1 query_worker_1 source_shard_a target_shard_a external_index_primary)
(attach_schema_to_participant indexing_worker_1 schema_v1)
(validate_index_with_indexer external_index_primary indexing_worker_1 schema_v1)
(activate_indexing_worker_for_index indexing_worker_1 external_index_primary)
(activate_query_worker_for_index query_worker_1 external_index_primary)
(detach_schema_from_participant indexing_worker_1 schema_v1)
(attach_schema_to_participant indexing_worker_1 schema_v1)
(attach_trace_to_participant indexing_worker_1 trace_token_1 schema_v1)
(activate_indexer_and_release_endpoint indexing_worker_1 search_endpoint_1 trace_token_1)
(detach_schema_from_participant indexing_worker_1 schema_v1)
(attach_schema_to_participant query_worker_1 schema_v1)
(attach_trace_to_participant query_worker_1 trace_token_1 schema_v1)
(activate_query_worker_and_release_endpoint query_worker_1 search_endpoint_2 trace_token_1)
(detach_schema_from_participant query_worker_1 schema_v1)
(register_participant pipeline_ingest_v1)
(bind_participant_to_endpoint pipeline_ingest_v1 search_endpoint_1)
(attach_schema_to_participant pipeline_ingest_v1 schema_v1)
(finalize_participant_registration pipeline_ingest_v1 schema_v1)
(stage_document_for_pipeline pipeline_ingest_v1 document_1 external_index_primary)
(start_pipeline_document_processing pipeline_ingest_v1 document_1 external_index_primary schema_v1)
(allocate_compute_to_pipeline pipeline_ingest_v1 compute_resource_slot_1)
(attach_credential_to_participant pipeline_ingest_v1 operator_credential_1)
(allocate_compute_and_start_pipeline pipeline_ingest_v1 compute_resource_slot_1 operator_credential_1 document_1 external_index_primary)
(attach_build_artifact_to_pipeline pipeline_ingest_v1 build_artifact_v1)
(attach_build_artifact_and_prepare_pipeline pipeline_ingest_v1 build_artifact_v1 document_1 external_index_primary)
(finalize_pipeline_staging pipeline_ingest_v1)
(attach_trace_to_participant pipeline_ingest_v1 trace_token_1 schema_v1)
(activate_participant_and_release_endpoint pipeline_ingest_v1 search_endpoint_1 trace_token_1)
(detach_schema_from_participant pipeline_ingest_v1 schema_v1)
(detach_credential_from_participant pipeline_ingest_v1 operator_credential_1)
(release_compute_from_pipeline pipeline_ingest_v1 compute_resource_slot_1)
(detach_build_artifact_from_pipeline pipeline_ingest_v1 build_artifact_v1)
(register_participant pipeline_sync_v1)
(bind_participant_to_endpoint pipeline_sync_v1 search_endpoint_3)
(attach_schema_to_participant pipeline_sync_v1 schema_v1)
(finalize_participant_registration pipeline_sync_v1 schema_v1)
(stage_document_for_pipeline pipeline_sync_v1 document_2 external_index_primary)
(start_pipeline_document_processing pipeline_sync_v1 document_2 external_index_primary schema_v1)
(allocate_compute_to_pipeline pipeline_sync_v1 compute_resource_slot_1)
(attach_credential_to_participant pipeline_sync_v1 operator_credential_1)
(allocate_compute_and_start_pipeline pipeline_sync_v1 compute_resource_slot_1 operator_credential_1 document_2 external_index_primary)
(attach_build_artifact_to_pipeline pipeline_sync_v1 build_artifact_v1)
(attach_build_artifact_and_prepare_pipeline pipeline_sync_v1 build_artifact_v1 document_2 external_index_primary)
(finalize_pipeline_staging pipeline_sync_v1)
(attach_trace_to_participant pipeline_sync_v1 trace_token_1 schema_v1)
(activate_participant_and_release_endpoint pipeline_sync_v1 search_endpoint_3 trace_token_1)
```
message.reasoning:
The problem is a classical planning problem in PDDL for external search engine integration. The goal is to activate four participants: indexing_worker_1, query_worker_1, pipeline_ingest_v1, and pipeline_sync_v1.


## Investigating problem scope  
- The task is a classical planning problem in PDDL, focusing on external search engine integration.  
- The goal is to activate four specific participants: indexing_worker_1, query_worker_1, pipeline_ingest_v1, and pipeline_sync_v1.

## Investigating problem scope  
- The task involves activating four participants: indexing_worker_1, query_worker_1, pipeline_ingest_v1, and pipeline_sync_v1, with additional components like schema_v1, operator_credential_1, and document_batch_1.  
- It includes managing environments (env_staging, env_production), shards (source_shard_a, target_shard_a), and external_index_primary, along with plugins (analyzer_1, tokenizer_1) and rankings (default, custom).  

## Examining activation process  
- Activation requires registering participants first, as none are registered initially, using actions like (register_participant indexing_worker_1).  
- Participants need endpoints for activation, but with only three available, workers may need to activate first to release endpoints for pipelines.  

## Analyzing worker readiness  
- To make workers ready, steps include binding endpoints, attaching schemas, finalizing registration, and claiming shards like source_shard_a.  
- Shard claiming requires participant readiness, credential binding, and shard readiness, achieved through actions like claim_indexer_for_shard.  

## Exploring index provisioning  
- Index provisioning needs workers ready and shards claimed, involving actions like validate_source_shard_for_indexer to ensure shard readiness.  
- This process also requires trace attachment and observability readiness, ensuring the index is fully set up for operation.

## Continuing worker activation  
- After making workers ready, detach credentials and schemas to free resources, like detaching schema_v1 from indexing_worker_1.  
- Both indexing_worker_1 and query_worker_1 are now ready, with shards claimed and resources available for further steps.  

## Provisioning external index  
- Provision the external index using both workers and shards, ensuring external_index_primary is assigned and provisioned.  
- Validate the index with indexing_worker_1 and re-attach schema_v1 temporarily for commit readiness, then detach it again.  

## Activating observability  
- Activate observability for both workers, requiring them to be ready, shards claimed, provisioned, and commit-ready.  
- This step ensures both indexing_worker_1 and query_worker_1 are observability-ready for further operations.  

## Managing trace attachment  
- Attach trace_token_1 to indexing_worker_1 first, consuming it, then activate and release both the endpoint and trace for reuse.  
- Repeat the process for query_worker_1, re-attaching schema_v1 as needed to complete activation and resource release.

## Investigating pipeline readiness  
- Pipelines need to be ready before staging documents, involving registration, endpoint binding, and schema attachment.  
- With endpoint_3 available and schemas/credentials shareable, pipelines like ingest_v1 can leverage existing resources.  

## Exploring compute allocation  
- Compute allocation is needed for pipeline operations, achieved through actions like allocate_compute_and_start_pipeline.  
- This step requires stage_ready, compute_attached, credential_binding, and document assignment, but no environment binding is necessary for some paths.  

## Assessing environment constraints  
- Ingest_v1 can use env_staging, which is available and tagged, but sync_v1 faces issues as env_production is not available.  
- Alternative paths like attach_build_artifact_and_prepare_pipeline bypass environment needs, relying on document staging and compute allocation instead.

## Managing pipeline sequencing  
- For pipeline_ingest_v1 with document_1, start by registering and binding it to search_endpoint_1, leveraging available endpoints.  
- After activation, ensure schema_v1 is detached from indexing_worker_1 and query_worker_1 to make it available for reuse.  

## Handling resource sharing  
- Second pipeline, sync_v1, can use document_2 and bypass environment needs, relying on document staging and compute allocation.  
- Release compute_slot_1 and build_artifact_v1 from the first pipeline to make them available for sync_v1, ensuring resource sharing.  

## Ensuring schema availability  
- After activating workers, schemas remain bound; detach schema_v1 from indexing_worker_1 post-activation to free it.  
- Reattach schema_v1 to query_worker_1 for trace attachment, then detach again to keep it available for pipeline operations.

## Managing worker setup  
- Indexing_worker_1 attaches to source_shard_a and schema_v1, then claims with operator_credential_1 for shard indexing.

pddl2nl_prompt.note: autoplanbench_pddl2nl: run_save_descriptions.py --type full — шаблоны/JSON, без вызова LLM на построение промпта для сэмпла.

domain_setup.note: Файл domain_llm_usage_seedN.json не найден для section/subsection этого id (старый autoplanbench или домен не пересобирали). После run_domain_setup с обновлённым autoplanbench файл появится рядом с domain_description_seedN.json.

pipeline_usage.totals_scope_note: Per-run totals = planning + pddl2nl_prompt + nl2pddl (только tracked=true). domain_setup всегда вне totals (included_in_per_run_totals=false); цифры в блоке — для справки, агрегировать отдельно: domain_llm_usage_seed*.json + дедуп в collect_pipeline_token_usage.py.
